name: Pot Stickers
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the flours and warm water. Knead for 15 minutes, adding more flour to water as needed to create a smooth, pliable dough. Cover the dough tightly with plastic wrap and let rest for 20 minutes.
2. Heat the sherry in a wok over medium heat. Stir-fry the onion and cabbage until limp. Add the remaining pot sticker ingredients. Cook, stirring, for 2 minutes.
3. Remove the mixture from the heat and place it in a colander to drain excess moisture. Divide the dough into 24 small balls. On a lightly floured board, flatten each ball of dough into a 3-4 inch circle.
4. Fill each circle with about 1 tbsp filling. Fold the circle into a half-moon shape; pinch the edges to seal.
5. Lightly coat two large nonstick skillets (or work in batches with one skillet) with vegetable cooking spray. Set over medium heat. When hot, add the pot stickers seam side up, flattening slightly on the bottom. When the bottoms of the pot stickers are golden brown, add 1/2 cup water to each pan.
6. Cover and steam for 20 minutes.
7. Makes 24 pot stickers.
NOTE: I usually make this recipe using the pot sticker "skins" that I find in the produce section of my supermarket. They probably use all white flour, so I can't vouch for their healthiness, but they do save time. To freeze, place the pot stickers in a single layer on a cookie sheet and let them completely freeze before storing them in ziploc bags or Tupperware containers. To cook, just add the frozen pot stickers to the pan and proceed as usual.
Woops, almost forgot: Each pot sticker has approximately 44 calories, 2 grams of protein, 0.2 grams of fat, 8 grams of carbohydrates, 0 cholesterol, 190 milligrams of sodium, and 1 gram of fiber.
